rows: AUTHORITY: LOCAL PLANNING SCHEME NO.4 & PLANNING AND
To establish sustainable building design requirements for new development.
This policy applies to all development requiring planning approval under the planning
a) Development subject to the provisions of State Planning Policy 7.3 –
b) Buildings used for any industrial use class under the heading Industrial Use
Classes in zoning table 2 of the Local Planning Scheme No. 4 (LPS4);
c) Any development with a Gross Lettable Area (GLA) of less than 1000 m² GLA;
d) Refurbishments of existing buildings over 1000m² GLA not involving
substantial structural or internal alteration and all refurbishments to buildings
e) Development not involving a physical change to a building;
g) Development subject to planning scheme, policy or structure plan provisions
which specify a specific or higher sustainability standard.
1. All development subject to this policy shall be designed and constructed in such a
a) A rating not less than 4 Star Green Star using the relevant Green Building
Council of Australia Green Star rating tool, or its equivalent demonstrated
through a report provided by a suitability qualified professional*.
* This may include a One Planet Living Action Plan that is certified by
Bioregional Australia or a One Planet Living Integrator.
2. Council may exercise discretion to waive or vary the requirements of the policy in
a) Development involves refurbishment of a building included on the Heritage List
or in a Heritage Area where, in the opinion of the Council, adherence to the
requirements of clause 1 would detrimentally impact on the heritage values of
b) No suitable sustainability rating tool has yet been developed for assessment of
the type of development proposed but it demonstrates a higher than standard
3. An application subject to this policy shall be accompanied by an outline of how the
4. Unless the Council waives any particular requirement, an application subject to this
policy may be made subject to a condition of approval that:
a) Prior to the issue of a building permit, the applicant/owner is to submit a copy
of documentation from the Green Building Council of Australia or a suitably
qualified professional stating how the development will achieve a Green Star
rating of at least 4 Stars or equivalent, to the satisfaction of the City of
b) Prior to occupation, the applicant/owner is to submit a copy of documentation
from the Green Building Council of Australia or a suitably qualified professional
stating that the development as constructed achieves a Green Star rating of at
least 4 Stars or equivalent, to the satisfaction of the City of Fremantle.
